revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24441147838|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八王子神社 (刈谷市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hachioji Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Hachioji-jinja ac (2).jpg | location = {{ill|Kariya, Aichi|en|Kariya, Aichi|qid=Q389515|lt=Kariya|1=Kariya, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Izumida-cho Nakanishi 96 | ISO = JP-23 | latitude_deg = 35 | latitude_min = 01 | latitude_sec = 18.82 | longitude_deg = 137 | longitude_min = 00 | longitude_sec = 07.7 | deities = {{ill|Hachiman|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}, Ōhirume no Mikoto ({{ill|Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja|大郎子命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarashinakatsuhiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}), {{ill|Mononobe no Soshin|ja|物部祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Soshin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135504559}} | status = Village Shrine | festival = Second Sunday of October | location = {{ill|Kariya|qid=Q389515|1=Kariya, Aichi}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.02188888888889|N|137.00213888888888|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
'''Hachioji Shrine''' (八王子神社, Hachioji Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Izumida-cho|ja|泉田町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumida-cho|1=izumidachou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18987927}}, {{ill|Kariya, Aichi|en|Kariya, Aichi|qid=Q389515|lt=Kariya|1=Kariya, Aichi}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its historical Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a village shrine.
== deity == * {{ill|Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|ja|国狭槌尊|1=Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
== History == The exact date of the shrine's foundation is unknown.<ref name="Yusho">"Yusho" (Historical Records), Hachioji Shrine.</ref> The shrine building was constructed in August 1173.<ref name="Yusho"/> Subsequent reconstructions occurred in {{ill|Eiroku|en|Eiroku|qid=Q1143341|lt=Eiroku|1=Eiroku}} 2 (1559) in September and in {{ill|Meireki|en|Meireki|qid=Q1192766|lt=Meireki|1=Meireki}}元年 (1655) on November 16.<ref name="Yusho"/>
In the aftermath of the {{ill|Typhoon_Vera|en|Typhoon_Vera|qid=Q1060156|lt=Typhoon Vera|1=Typhoon Vera}} on September 26, 1959, the shrine sustained significant damage, including the loss of ancient cedar trees.<ref name="Yusho"/> In 1872, it was designated as a village shrine under the shrine Ranking system.<ref name="Yusho"/>
== Grounds == * Shrine Hall * Shrine Office * Preservation Hall * Purification Pavilion * Chōzuya (Water Ablution Pavilion) * Stone Monuments ** Jinchikuseki (Earth-Tamping Stone) and Chikaraishi (Power Stone) ** "Memorial to Fallen Soldiers" ** "Monument to Soldiers of the Russo-Japanese War" * Hachioji Shrine Shell Mound * Izumida Kofun (Ancient Kofun) ** Excavated in December 1960 from a field in Izumida-cho Miyahigashi, 200 meters southeast of Hachioji Shrine. While the tumulus was destroyed during agricultural development, artifacts such as two human skeletons, five gold rings, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, and iron arrowheads were recovered. Dating back to the mid-to-late 6th century, the site featured a {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} with stones forming its walls. A reconstructed stone chamber now stands on the shrine grounds, with the original artifacts preserved in the {{ill|Kariya City Museum|ja|刈谷市郷土資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariya City Museum|1=Kariya City Museum of Local History|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17226430}}.
<gallery> File:Hachioji-jinja ac (1).jpg|Torii gate File:Kariya Izumida Kofun ac.jpg|Izumida Kofun </gallery>
=== Subsidiary Shrines === * Yama-no-Kami Shrine * Itsukushima Shrine * Hachiman Shrine * Akiba Shrine
== Cultural Properties == === Municipal Historic Sites === * Hachioji Shrine Shell Mound ** Designated on November 11, 1958.<ref>[https://www.city.kariya.lg.jp/kankobunka/rekishibunka/bunkazai_iseki/bunkazaishitei.html Kariya City Cultural Properties List], Kariya City.</ref> The site represents a {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|qid=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period|1=Yayoi period}} shell mound. Kariya City hosts other significant archaeological sites, including the Yama-no-Kami Site (Paleolithic, located on the grounds of {{ill|Kariya East Junior High School|ja|刈谷市立刈谷東中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kariya East Junior High School|1=Kariya East Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24514395}}), Honkariya Shell Mound ({{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}, located at Honkariya Shrine), and Izumida Kofun (Kofun period).
== Visitor Information == === location === * Address: Izumida-cho Nakanishi 96, Kariya City, Aichi Prefecture, Japan.
=== Access === * By Rail: Approximately a short walk from {{ill|Fujimatsu Station|en|Fujimatsu Station|qid=Q5507435|lt=Fujimatsu Station|1=Fujimatsu Station}}, served by the {{ill|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|en|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|qid=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kariya City's Kofuns: Supplement to the Kariya City Historical Records,'' Cultural Property Preservation Committee & Local History Research Association, Kariya City, 1964.
{{Shinto}}
<
